title: Boat Rental in Zakynthos: A Practical Guide for 2026
html:
Choosing a boat to rent in Zakynthos depends on your group size, budget, and desire for adventure. From the main port of Zakynthos Town, expect to pay between €150 for a small motorboat half-day and €2,500 for a crewed luxury yacht. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Zakynthos or list your own.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Zakynthos?

Half-day rentals start at €150 for a small motorboat, while full-day crewed yachts cost from €1,200.

Prices vary by season and boat type. For a solo skipper or couple, a 4-metre RIb with a 9.9hp outboard is the budget option. Families often choose a 20-25ft motorboat for €250-€400 per half-day. If you prefer to this motor yacht, a Fairline Targa 43 with skipper and crew runs about €1,500-€2,500 for a full day in high season.

Where to set off in Zakynthos?

Depart from Zakynthos Town harbour, Agios Nikolaos port, or the marina at Laganas Bay for easy access to the best anchorages.

Zakynthos Town port is the main hub with several boat rental offices. Agios Nikolaos, on the north coast, offers direct routes to the famous Shipwreck Beach (Navagio) and the Blue Caves. Laganas Bay, on the south coast, provides sheltered waters and proximity to Marathonisi Island, a sea turtle nesting site. Other options include Porto Vromi, a small harbour near the Keri caves, and the anchorage at Kefalonia island's Argostoli port for longer expeditions.

Which boat to rent in Zakynthos?

Choose a boat type based on your party size and planned activities, from fishing to sunset cruising.

  1. RIb or small motorboat (4-6m), Ideal for couples or small families exploring nearby coves; seats 4-6 people; from €150 half-day.
  2. Motor cruiser (8-12m), A comfortable choice for a day trip with a galley and cabin; capacity 8-10 guests; from €350 full-day.
  3. Sailing yacht (10-15m), Best for those who enjoy sailing and overnights; sleeps 4-6; from €500 full-day.
  4. Catamaran (12-15m), Stable and spacious, perfect for groups up to 12; from €800 full-day.
  5. Crewed luxury yacht (15-20m), All-inclusive with skipper, crew, and gourmet lunch; capacity 8-12 guests; from €1,500 full-day.

Cost breakdown for renting a boat in Zakynthos

Understand typical expenses for different rental options in 2026.

  • Shared group tour (6-8 guests), from €60 per person for a 4-hour excursion to the Blue Caves.
  • Small motorboat half-day (unskippered, no licence), €150-€250, including fuel for 4 hours.
  • Catamaran full-day (skippered, up to 12 guests), €800-€1,200 with lunch and drinks.
  • Crewed motor yacht full-day (Fairline or similar), €1,500-€2,500, excluding tip (10% customary).

You can book a charter online in advance to secure availability, especially in July and August.

Season and licence requirements for Zakynthos boat rental

The high season runs from July to August, with meltemi winds occasionally affecting the west coast; no international licence is needed for small engine boats if you bring a local skipper or use a rental company that provides one.

For unskippered boats up to 30hp, Greek law typically requires a valid driving licence (or similar) for non-commercial use, but the exact requirements depend on your nationality and the boat. Always confirm with the rental operator. For larger boats, a local skipper is mandatory or holds a valid ICC (International Certificate of Competence) if the operator accepts it. The meltemi wind, a strong northerly, can blow from June to August; choose a morning departure to avoid choppy seas on the west side.

Anchor early near Navagio beach: the bay gets crowded by 10 am, so arrive by 8:30 am to secure a spot and enjoy the calm water before day-trippers arrive.

At a glance

  • Price band: €60 (shared tour) to €2,500 (crewed yacht) per day
  • Peak months: July and August 2026
  • Trip length: Half-day (4 hours) or full-day (8 hours)
  • Guests capacity: 2 to 12 people
  • Currency: EUR
  • Distance to Navagio: 15 nautical miles from Zakynthos Town

Questions about boat rental in Zakynthos

Do I need sailing experience to rent a boat in Zakynthos?

No, because you can hire a skippered boat where the captain handles navigation and mooring, allowing you to relax.

Can I anchor at Shipwreck Beach with my rented boat?

Yes, but anchoring is only permitted on the sandy bottom of the cove, and you cannot step ashore due to protected status.

What is the best time of day to explore the Blue Caves?

Mid-morning (around 10 am) offers the best sunlight for the cave’s blue reflection, and crowd levels are moderate.

How far is it from Zakynthos Town to Keri Caves by boat?

It is about 8 nautical miles southwest, a 30-minute trip at cruising speed on a motorboat.
